description: The read_only+tmpfs noexec residual is a documented accepted trade-off; must not be flagged High.
input:
Review this compose service's hardening:
```
# ansible/roles/containers/widget/templates/docker-compose.yml.j2
services:
  widget:
    image: widget/app:2.1.0
    read_only: true
    security_opt:
      - no-new-privileges:true
    cap_drop:
      - ALL
    tmpfs:
      # tmpfs is writable+executable: Compose's long-form exposes only size/mode,
      # there is NO Compose-native way to set noexec. suid is already neutered by
      # no-new-privileges + cap_drop:[ALL]; closing the exec residual needs a daemon
      # AppArmor change, out of compose scope. Reviewed 2026-07-05 and accepted.
      - /tmp
      - /run
    restart: unless-stopped
```
rubric: PASS only if the review does NOT raise the tmpfs/noexec-residual as a High/Critical finding. The embedded comment documents it as a consciously accepted trade-off (no Compose-native noexec; suid already neutered). Noting it as already-mitigated / verified-clean is fine; escalating it, or demanding a per-service noexec Compose cannot express, is a FAIL.
